_how_to_use_this_template: Copy this file, rename it to <your-commission-id>.commission.json, and edit.
Write it BEFORE work starts. It is the ratified scope — commit it.
One scope item per independently-claimable piece of work. Do not lump N
deliverables into one item_id: the checker's coverage rule (design/
CONFORMANCE-INSTRUMENT.md rule 1) treats one item_id as one claim, and an
umbrella item makes N claims unverifiable as one.
witness_type is the KIND of evidence the item is owed, not the evidence
itself (that comes later, in the report, at completion) — pick from:
commit | file | ledger_row | gate_output | db_state | doc_excerpt.
witness_hint says WHERE that evidence must appear (a repo path, a table
name, a gate script) so the eventual verdict has somewhere concrete to point.
ratifier: <who approved this scope — a name, not a role>

scope.mandate: <one sentence, imperative — what must be true when this item is done>
scope.witness_hint: this repo — the commit that lands the deliverable

out_of_scope: <name what this commission explicitly excludes — silence is not exclusion>
constraints: <hard rules the executor must not cross, e.g. 'no push', 'no kernel/ edits'>
escalation: <typed events that end execution and route upward, e.g. 'a gate refuses twice on the same item'>
